titleStmt.title: Letter from R.E. Shanahan to Cornelius Daly, 16 March 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: R E Shanahan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Castleisland March 16th '23 My Dear Con, Wife & Family In this awful time
of your Sad Bereavement, Myself & Family
join in sending you our Heartfelt Sorrow
for the loss of your Brave Noble Boy
Poor Charlie (R.I.P) To know him was to love him. Ah. God
how Cruel. I thought Ireland never reared
a Son that would send Poor Charlie to his
Doom. But God knows best, Con. I know
on this occasion words Bear little Meaning
For a Brave Old Heat. Such as yours
that is stabbed to the Core
Ah. Ireland. Ireland. Ireland. Such is the fate
of your Noblest Best.
As one who loved Charlie and Knowing you
Idolized him such as I did my own Brave
'Bouchal Bawn' Poor. Poor. Dick. R.I.P.
Knowing & Loving them Both. Is it not a consolation
to you & I to know they Never shrank From
giving up their lives for 'The Old Land'
The Fondest wish of Their Hearts would be Oh, How Sweet to Live & Love Her,
Grander still it were to die,
With The Green Clad Hills of Ireland,
Echoing out their Last 'Good-Bye' God Comfort you all is the
Prayer of your old Friend R.E. Shanahan
